title: Correlated subquery selecting max/min
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.posts_at_top_score/0` that returns posts whose `score` equals the maximum score for their category. Use a correlated subquery: `where: p.score == subquery(max_for_category)` where `max_for_category` selects `max(p2.score) from Post p2 where p2.category_id == parent_as(:post).category_id`. Name the outer binding `as: :post`. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: Combining correlated subquery + aggregate is subtle; `parent_as` must carry the category_id correlation.
